reasoning: Based on comprehensive search results up to May 16, 2025, the US Steel/Nippon Steel merger has been blocked by Biden executive order since January 3, 2025. Key facts: 1) CFIUS extension requires parties to permanently abandon transaction by June 18, 2025; 2) Both companies are litigating and have not voluntarily terminated; 3) No 8-K filing of termination found; 4) Legal proceedings ongoing with DOJ requesting briefing deadline extensions through mid-May 2025; 5) Deadline is only 13 days after June 18 cutoff. Given the executive order requiring abandonment, the imminent deadline, and no evidence of settlement or extension, a 72% probability reflects high likelihood that formal termination via 8-K will be filed before July 1, even if companies continue legal challenges. The primary uncertainty is whether deadline could be extended again or if litigation success is possible, but base rates for overcoming presidential blocking orders are very low.
